action_choice_hints: The candidate action cell is adjacent to a cell with a special directional marker.
The candidate action cell is in the same row or column as a target object, with no blocking obstacles in between.
The candidate action cell is adjacent to a previously marked or modified cell.
The candidate action cell is at the boundary of a contiguous region of empty cells.
The candidate action cell is closer to a target object than other available cells, considering obstacles.
feature_family_hints: Adjacency relations: Needed to determine if the candidate action cell is next to important objects or markers.
Line-of-sight features: Required to assess whether there is an unobstructed path between the candidate cell and a target object.
Region boundary detection: To identify if the candidate cell lies at the edge of a region of interest.
Relative distance features: To compare the proximity of candidate cells to target objects, accounting for obstacles.
